---
title: "Which cohort calibrates our immunogenicity ranker — and does it transfer to splice-junction neoepitopes?"
subtitle: "Research decision · Issue #592 · gate for Issue #547 · 2026-05-31"
author: "Jin-Ho Lee"
institute: "Splice Neoepitope Pipeline · JH M Lee Lab"
date: 2026-05-31
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [simple, ../../slides/custom.scss]
    footer: "Splice Neoepitope Pipeline · JH M Lee Lab"
    incremental: false
    slide-number: c/t
    progress: true
    chalkboard: false
    code-line-numbers: false
    fig-align: center
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    transition: fade
    background-transition: fade
    width: 1280
    height: 720
# PDF (beamer) render disabled — same Quarto/pandoc longtable issue as the pilot deck.
# HTML reveal.js is the primary delivery; "Print → Save as PDF" in Chrome for handouts.
bibliography: refs.bib
csl: ../../slides/nature.csl
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

## The question

::: {style="text-align: center; font-size: 1.8em; font-weight: bold; line-height: 1.2; margin-top: 0.4em;"}
Which labelled cohort\
calibrates our immunogenicity ranker —\
and may we apply it to *splice-junction* neoepitopes?
:::

. . .

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**Why now:** [Issue #547](https://github.com/Jin-HoMLee/splice-neoepitope-pipeline/issues/547) (NeoGuider-style KDE + centered-isotonic calibration [@wei2025neoguider; @oron2017isotonic]) cannot start until a training cohort is chosen and the transfer question is settled. This deck lands that gate decision.
:::

::: {style="font-size: 0.6em; color: #777; margin-top: 0.4em;"}
**Terminology:** "splice-junction(-derived) neoepitope" throughout = a peptide translated across a tumor-specific **neojunction** (an *RNA-level* aberrant exon–exon junction). Distinct from **proteasome-spliced peptides** (post-translational fragment re-ligation) — not the subject here.
:::

---

## The gate — what blocks Issue #547 {.smaller}

MHCflurry's `presentation_score` [@odonnell2020mhcflurry2] answers *"will this peptide be displayed?"* — **not** *"will it provoke a T-cell?"* [Issue #547](https://github.com/Jin-HoMLee/splice-neoepitope-pipeline/issues/547) learns the display → immunogenicity mapping. To *learn* it, we need labelled examples.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A["MHCflurry<br/>presentation_score"] --> B["calibration layer<br/>(KDE + centered isotonic)"]
  B --> C["calibrated<br/>immunogenicity log-odds"]
  T["labelled training cohort<br/>(peptide + immunogenic? + HLA)"] -.trains.-> B
  style B fill:#1e5ba8,color:#fff
  style T fill:#b8860b,color:#fff
```

::: {.callout-important appearance="simple"}
Two scientific questions had to be answered first: **(1) which cohort?** and **(2) does an SNV/indel-trained calibration transfer to our splice-junction neoepitopes at all?**
:::

---

## The cohort landscape {.smaller}

All publicly-validated immunogenicity cohorts are **point-mutation (SNV/indel)** — there is no "splice-junction cohort" to pick among.

| Cohort | Tested / immunogenic | Variant class | Access |
|---|---|---|---|
| **NeoRanking harmonized** [@mueller2023neoranking]<br/>(NCI-train + TESLA + HiTIDE + Bjerregaard) | ~74 pts / **~165 pos** | SNV/indel | **Open** figshare; ⚠️ LICR copyright |
| **Borch / IMPROVE** [@borch2024improve] | 17,520 / **467 pos** | SNV/indel | **Open** |
| TESLA alone [@wells2020tesla] | 608 / 37 pos | SNV/indel (splice *excluded*) | open (small) |
| ~~Borch *eLife* viral multimer~~ | — | viral, 0 neoantigens | ⚠️ wrong paper |

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**Pick:** NeoRanking harmonized pool **+ IMPROVE** — already harmonized to peptide + label + HLA tables; pooling beats any single cohort given sparse positives.
:::

---

## Does it transfer to splice-junction neoepitopes? {.smaller}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**FOR transfer**

::: {.incremental}
- Presentation depends on **sequence + HLA**, not variant origin
- Splice-junction-derived candidates show **comparable predicted MHC binding ranks** to SNVs in-cohort [@lang2024splice2neo]
- Field already does it — **SNAF runs MHCflurry + DeepImmuno unchanged** on splice-junction-derived peptides [@kahles2024snaf]
:::
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**AGAINST blind transfer**

::: {.incremental}
- Splice-junction-derived peptides are **less self-similar to self** → under-call [@lang2024splice2neo]
- Splice-junction neoantigens are **shared / recurrent** (≤90% melanoma), distinct AA composition [@kahles2024snaf]
- **No published empirical test** on validated splice-junction-derived peptides — both sides inferential
:::
:::
:::

. . .

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
**Net:** the bias is **directional and conservative (under-call), not random** — the safe failure mode for a ranker. The MVP calibrates *presentation only*, the most origin-agnostic link.
:::

---

## Two corrections the fact-check caught {.smaller}

A 109-agent deep-research sweep (26 sources, 80 claims → 25 adversarially verified) — two load-bearing claims were corrected against primary sources before being trusted:

::: {.incremental}
1. **Self-similarity direction *flipped*.** Prior intuition: splice-junction-derived peptides look *more* self-like → calibration would *over*-predict. Literature [@lang2024splice2neo]: the **opposite** — splice-junction-derived MHC-I candidates are *less* self-similar → SNV-trained curve **under**-predicts. (Strengthens the proceed case.)
2. **License.** Synthesis said NeoRanking was "MIT." Direct repo check: **no OSS license — LICR/Ludwig copyright.** Consume for training; **do not redistribute** raw tables in-repo.
:::

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
Verification changed the conclusion's *basis*, not just its confidence — the whole reason to fact-check.
:::

---

## Hold-out design {.smaller}

Resolves [Issue #547](https://github.com/Jin-HoMLee/splice-neoepitope-pipeline/issues/547) open-question #3.

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="52%"}
**Leave-one-cohort-out** ✅

Train on TESLA + HiTIDE + NCI → test on IMPROVE; rotate. Measures the real risk: **cohort-to-cohort drift**.
:::
::: {.column width="48%"}
**Within-cohort k-fold** ❌

Shuffles inside one cohort — **hides** exactly the drift we care about. Over-optimistic.
:::
:::

. . .

::: {.callout-important appearance="simple"}
No splice-junction-specific labels exist at scale (**~5 validated positives** field-wide [@kahles2024snaf]) → waiting would stall [Issue #547](https://github.com/Jin-HoMLee/splice-neoepitope-pipeline/issues/547) indefinitely. **Proceed now.**
:::

---

## Decision {.smaller}

::: {style="text-align: center; font-size: 2.0em; font-weight: bold; margin-top: 0.1em;"}
Proceed now — qualified yes
:::

::: {style="text-align: center; font-size: 1.1em; margin-top: 0.1em;"}
NeoRanking harmonized + IMPROVE · leave-one-cohort-out · presentation-anchored prior
:::

. . .

- **Output is a *presentation-anchored prior*, not a validated probability** — label the column so.
- **Forward link:** the documented splice-junction under-call is exactly what a foreignness / dissimilarity-to-self term corrects → [Issue #585](https://github.com/Jin-HoMLee/splice-neoepitope-pipeline/issues/585) (Łuksza foreignness) becomes the natural splice-junction-correction follow-up.
- **Open AC before code:** confirm the figshare tables actually download + schema-check.

---

## Caveats — read before citing {.smaller}

::: {.incremental}
1. **Transfer is qualified, not proven** — no direct empirical splice-junction test exists; both arguments inferential.
2. **License** — NeoRanking is LICR copyright (no OSS license); train on it, don't redistribute raw tables.
3. **Label scarcity** — positives sparse even pooled (TESLA 37, IMPROVE 467, NCI-train 82, HiTIDE 41, Bjerregaard 8); the splice-junction tail is unrepresented.
4. **Splice-junction output = prior, not probability** — until a validated cohort-level calibrator exists.
5. **Pilot deck** — first *research-decision* deck (vs eval / experiment tiers); convention itself under evaluation.
:::

---

## References {.smaller}

::: {#refs}
:::
